content.formatted_body:
I'm starting to write some tests for classes scopes, and I have one question. For a code like:

class C {
  field;
  method() { ... }
}

let c = new C();
c.method();

How would be your expectation for C's scope here? Would it include on its variables field [method, field]? Right now I'm thinking it would be something like:

{
  start: { line: 0, column: 0 },
  end: { line: 4, column: 1 },
  kind: "global",
  isStackFrame: false,
  variables: ["Counter", "c"],
  children: [{
    start: { line: 0, column: 0 },
    end: { line: 3, column: 1 },
    kind: "class",
    name: "Counter",
    isStackFrame: false,
    variables: ["field", "method"],
    children: [{
      // ...
      name: "method",
      isStackFrame: true,
      variables: [...],
      children: [],
    }],
  }],
}

Does it make any sense?
